questions.question: What is the job of Kavya's father?
questions.explanation: Kavya's father buys wood and makes furniture to sell in the market.

questions.question: How does Sahil, a farm labourer, get paid?
questions.explanation: Sahil gets some payment in cash and the remaining part in the form of mangoes of equal value.

questions.question: What is a non-cash payment called?
questions.explanation: When workers receive goods or services as part of their wages instead of cash.

questions.question: Who receives a salary?
questions.correct_answer: Geeta Aunty, the Air Force pilot
questions.explanation: She serves the country and performs an economic activity too.

questions.question: What is Sevā?
questions.explanation: It involves doing good deeds without expecting anything in return.

questions.question: How do people exchange goods and services?
questions.correct_answer: In markets or through professional services
questions.explanation: People may exchange goods for other goods, but most often they are exchanged for money.

questions.question: What is value addition?
questions.correct_answer: Adding monetary value to a product or service
questions.explanation: It involves creating value by transforming something into another form.

questions.question: Who adds value to the wood by turning it into furniture?
questions.explanation: He uses his skill and time to create a valuable product from raw materials.

questions.question: What are non-economic activities?
questions.correct_answer: Activities that do not generate income or wealth but are done out of feelings like gratitude, love, care, and respect
questions.explanation: Examples include parents cooking food for the family or helping their children with schoolwork.

questions.question: What is a community programme?
questions.correct_answer: A collective effort by members of the community to achieve a common goal
questions.explanation: Examples include tree plantation drives, cleaning public places, and teaching computer skills to young adults.

important_notes.title: Economic Activities
important_notes.content: Involves money or is performed in exchange for money or money's worth. Examples: business person selling school bags, farmer selling produce, lawyer arguing a case.

important_notes.title: Non-Economic Activities
important_notes.content: Do not generate income or wealth but are done out of feelings like gratitude, love, care, and respect. Examples: parents cooking food for the family, helping children with schoolwork.

important_notes.title: Value Addition
important_notes.content: Involves creating value by transforming something into another form. Examples: carpenter turning wood into furniture, chef preparing a meal from raw ingredients.

important_notes.title: Payment in Kind
important_notes.content: When workers receive goods or services as part of their wages instead of cash. Example: Sahil receiving mangoes as part of his payment.

important_notes.title: Sevā (Selfless Service)
important_notes.content: Involves doing good deeds without expecting anything in return. Examples: volunteering at a community programme, helping a neighbour with their chores.

important_notes.title: Community Programme
important_notes.content: A collective effort by members of the community to achieve a common goal. Examples: tree plantation drives, cleaning public places, teaching computer skills to young adults.

important_notes.title: Payment in Cash
important_notes.content: When workers receive money as part of their wages. Example: Geeta Aunty receiving a monthly salary.

important_notes.title: Value of Human Labour
important_notes.content: Involves recognizing the value of human effort and time. Examples: paying for a service, hiring a worker to perform a task.

important_notes.title: Types of Economic Activities
important_notes.content: Include production, distribution, and exchange of goods and services. Examples: manufacturing, transportation, marketing.

important_notes.title: Importance of Non-Economic Activities
important_notes.content: They contribute to the well-being of individuals and society as a whole. Examples: volunteering, helping a neighbour, caring for family members.

facts.fact: Kavya's father is a carpenter who buys wood and makes furniture to sell in the market.
Sahil, a farm labourer, gets paid in cash and kind (mangoes).
Geeta Aunty receives a monthly salary as an Air Force pilot.
Sevā is selfless service that involves doing good deeds without expecting anything in return.
Community programmes are collective efforts by members of the community to achieve a common goal.
Payment in kind occurs when workers receive goods or services as part of their wages instead of cash.
Value addition involves creating value by transforming something into another form.
Kavya's aunt is a technician who operates a bulldozer and receives a monthly salary.
Rajesh, the carpenter, adds value to wood by turning it into furniture.
Non-economic activities contribute to the well-being of individuals and society as a whole.
